2605.25933.title: Quantitative Evaluation of the Severity of Posttraumatic Stress Disorder through Transfer Learning from Specific Phobia Data
2605.25933.paper_summary: Posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD) is a prevalent and debilitating mental health condition with significant personal and societal impacts. Current clinical assessments of PTSD often rely on subjective evaluations, which can be time-consuming, costly, and prone to human bias. This study proposes a machine learning (ML) approach based on multivariate kernel density estimation (MKDE) technique for the objective evaluation of PTSD severity. We collected heart rate (HR) and galvanic skin response (GSR) signals as well as PTSD Checklist - Military Version (PCL-M) labels from 21 participants during an immersive simulation. A fear-response model was trained on a public arachnophobia dataset, and predictive features of PTSD were extracted from the fear-response curves estimated on the military dataset. The model achieved an accuracy of 86\% in classifying PTSD status, effectively distinguishing participants with and without PTSD (PCL-M threshold of 36). The average mean absolute error (MAE) of the models is 5.6, and it estimated a clinical PTSD severity scale with a mean absolute percentage error of 17\%. Our algorithm demonstrates promising potential for enhancing estimation of PTSD severity and followup by offering an objective and low-effort evaluation approach using physiology. These findings suggest clinical utility in both screening and follow-up settings.
2605.25933.paper_summary_zh: 創傷後壓力症候群（PTSD）是一種普遍且削弱身心的心理健康狀況，對個人和社會都有重大影響。當前對PTSD的臨床評估通常依賴主觀評估，這可能耗時、費用高昂，並且容易受到人為偏見的影響。本研究提出了一種基於多變量核密度估計（MKDE）技術的機器學習（ML）方法，用於PTSD嚴重程度的客觀評估。我們在一個沉浸式模擬中收集了21名參與者的心率（HR）和皮膚電反應（GSR）信號，以及PTSD檢查表 - 軍事版本（PCL-M）標籤。基於公共的懼蛛症數據集訓練了一個恐懼反應模型，並從軍事數據集中估計的恐懼反應曲線中提取了PTSD的預測特徵。該模型在分類PTSD狀態方面達到了86\%的準確率，有效區分了有PTSD和無PTSD的參與者（PCL-M閾值為36）。模型的平均絕對誤差（MAE）為5.6，並估計臨床PTSD嚴重程度量表的平均絕對百分比誤差為17\%。我們的算法顯示出增強PTSD嚴重程度評估和後續跟進的良好潛力，通過使用生理學提供客觀且低努力的評估方法。這些發現表明在篩查和後續設置中具有臨床實用性。
2605.25933.author: Nicolas Ricka et.al.
2605.25933.authors: Nicolas Ricka, Gauthier Pellegrin, Denis A. Fompeyrine, Thomas Rohaly, Leah Enders, Heather Roy
